name: Bell Pepper
description: Sweet and mild, bell peppers have no heat at all. Perfect for salads and cooking.

name: Poblano
description: Mild pepper often used for chile rellenos. When dried, it's called an ancho pepper.

name: Jalapeño
description: A popular medium-heat pepper, commonly used in Mexican cuisine. Great for salsas and poppers.

name: Serrano
description: Hotter than jalapeño, serrano peppers are commonly used fresh in salsas and pico de gallo.

name: Cayenne
description: Often dried and ground into powder, cayenne adds significant heat to dishes.

name: Thai Chili
description: Small but mighty! Essential in Thai and Southeast Asian cooking.

name: Habanero
description: Extremely hot with a fruity flavor. Popular in hot sauces and Caribbean cuisine.

name: Scotch Bonnet
description: Similar heat to habanero with a sweet, tropical flavor. Essential in jerk seasoning.

name: Ghost Pepper
description: Also known as Bhut Jolokia, this was once the world's hottest pepper. Handle with extreme caution!

name: Carolina Reaper
description: Currently holds the Guinness World Record for hottest pepper. Not for the faint of heart!
